publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System — H.4.1 'Factors Affecting Reserve Balances of Depository Institutions and Condition Statement of Federal Reserve Banks'; redistributed by FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis) as series SWPT. Per-counterparty swap operation results are published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (Markets Group).
dataset_title: Central bank liquidity swaps (Fed H.4.1 balance-sheet asset line). FRED series SWPT = 'Assets: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Wednesday Level'.
role_in_matrix: Supplies the official-sector cross-border dollar-PROVISION leg of the FWTW matrix: the Federal Reserve's outstanding dollar liquidity injected to foreign central banks via central-bank liquidity swap lines (Fed -> foreign central bank, on-lent to foreign banks). This is the dollar EXPANSION of opposite sign to a reserve/funding run — the official circuit-breaker that historically arrested the 2008 (~$583bn) and 2020 (~$449bn) stress episodes. The outstanding swap balance is carried on the Fed balance sheet as a weekly STOCK/position at each release date, so it is in-scope for a stock/position matrix. STRESS-STATE dimension: at the matrix's 2025 reference snapshot the balance is ~$35mn (near-zero), so omitting it does NOT distort the present-day LEVEL of the matrix; the defect it fills is a STRUCTURAL/dynamics gap (the backstop that acts under stress). This contract is a data-grounding artifact only; it specifies no F4 / identification / separability content.
bilateral_or_marginals: marginal (system aggregate groundable; per-counterparty-central-bank breakdown UNVERIFIED — see structure.counterparty_breakdown and unverified).

structure.description: Single weekly time series: the outstanding dollar amount of central bank liquidity swaps on the Federal Reserve System balance sheet (an asset line of H.4.1, 'factors supplying reserve funds'). FRED series id SWPT. FRED /data/SWPT metadata (fetched 2026-06-29, HTTP 200) confirms verbatim: series title 'Assets: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Wednesday Level'; Release 'H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances'; Frequency 'Weekly, As of Wednesday'; Units 'Millions of U.S. Dollars'; Seasonal Adjustment 'Not Seasonally Adjusted'. The H.4.1 current release (fetched 2026-06-29) prints the line label 'Central bank liquidity swaps' with the current value $35 million for the week ended June 24, 2026.
structure.h41_line_item: VERIFIED. Line label as printed on https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h41/current/ (2026-06-29): 'Central bank liquidity swaps'. Current value $35 million, week ended June 24, 2026. This matches the FRED SWPT current CSV observation (2026-06-24 = 35), confirming SWPT is the FRED redistribution of this exact H.4.1 line.
structure.counterparty_breakdown: DETERMINATION (load-bearing): The groundable, machine-readable series (SWPT / H.4.1 'Central bank liquidity swaps') is the SYSTEM AGGREGATE only. VERIFIED from the H.4.1 current release page (2026-06-29): only a single aggregate figure is displayed; the supporting tables (incl. table 1A Memorandum Items) do NOT disaggregate the swap balance by individual foreign central bank counterparty. Therefore on H.4.1/FRED the series is AGGREGATE-ONLY. SEPARATELY, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (Markets Group) publishes 'central bank liquidity swap operations' results: the NY Fed desk-operations page (fetched 2026-06-29, HTTP 200) states verbatim 'The results of the central bank liquidity swap operations and small value exercises of the central bank liquidity swap lines are published on a weekly basis when conducted,' and references the NY Fed Markets API (markets.newyorkfed.org). Historically the NY Fed has published swap operation results / amounts outstanding by counterparty central bank (e.g. ECB, Bank of Japan, Swiss National Bank, Bank of England, Bank of Canada). HOWEVER, a machine-readable per-counterparty 'amounts outstanding by counterparty' data file / API endpoint could NOT be confirmed in this environment: the NY Fed central-bank-swap-arrangements page 403'd (WebFetch) / 302-redirected (curl); the desk-operations page and the Markets-API doc page are JS-rendered shells whose static HTML carries no swap data table or swap endpoint path. CONCLUSION: classify SWPT system aggregate as the groundable series; the per-counterparty-central-bank breakdown is UNVERIFIED and needs the NY Fed (Markets API / operation-results pages) to confirm machine-readability before it can be wired in as a bilateral cell.
structure.stock_not_flow: The series is a STOCK: the outstanding swap balance (Wednesday level) on the Fed balance sheet at each weekly date — the same stock/position type the matrix records. It is not merely a flow/backstop with no position counterpart.

access.method: Public, free, no authentication. Primary FRED CSV endpoint https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=SWPT retrieved via direct HTTP (curl, --cacert /root/.ccr/ca-bundle.crt) returning HTTP 200, 1228 data rows. FRED /data/SWPT text endpoint returned HTTP 200 (metadata table). Primary H.4.1 release page https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h41/current/ retrieved (WebFetch, 2026-06-29). The FRED /series/SWPT HTML page returns HTTP 403 via the proxy (consistent with Test C's note); metadata was obtained equivalently from the FRED /data/ text endpoint and CSV plus the H.4.1 release page.
access.api_or_bulk: FRED CSV bulk download confirmed working (fredgraph.csv?id=SWPT, HTTP 200). A FRED JSON/REST API (api.stlouisfed.org/fred) exists in general but was not exercised here (CSV sufficed and required no API key). For per-counterparty detail, the NY Fed Markets API (markets.newyorkfed.org) is referenced by the publisher but its swap endpoints were not confirmable from the JS-rendered doc shell (see unverified).

frequency: Weekly. VERIFIED from FRED /data/SWPT metadata (2026-06-29): Frequency 'Weekly, As of Wednesday'. Corroborated by the H.4.1 current release being a weekly publication ('Week ended June 24, 2026') and by weekly observation dates in the fetched CSV (consecutive Wednesdays, e.g. 2020-05-20, 2020-05-27, 2020-06-03). Not from memory.
reporting_lag: Short, weekly. H.4.1 is published weekly (typically Thursday for the week ended the prior Wednesday). VERIFIED empirically: on 2026-06-29 the current H.4.1 release and the FRED CSV both carried the week-ended/observation date 2026-06-24 (the preceding Wednesday), i.e. roughly a few-day lag. The Federal Reserve's exact stated release-schedule/lag policy was NOT read from a publisher schedule page and is recorded as UNVERIFIED.
coverage: VERIFIED from the fetched CSV: weekly observations from 2002-12-18 (value 0) through 2026-06-24 (value 35), 1228 data rows. Baseline 0 before the facility's use; first non-zero observation 2007-12-26 (14000). The window SPANS BOTH the 2008 and 2020 stress episodes. Observations confirmed present and dense around both stress peaks: the 2008-11 through 2009-01 block (e.g. 2008-12-10=582575, 2008-12-17=583135, 2008-12-24=552728) and the 2020-04 through 2020-06 block (e.g. 2020-05-20=446103, 2020-05-27=448946, 2020-06-03=446945). Note this fetched window (2002-2026) is WIDER than the 2007-2021 window Test C persisted, and is now itself persisted to build/data/fed_swaps/SWPT_2002_2026.csv so the window/current-value claims are re-checkable from disk.

peak_values.method: Computed directly from the fetched CSV (persisted to build/data/fed_swaps/SWPT_2002_2026.csv, 1228 weekly rows 2002-12-18..2026-06-24), not copied from Test C; reported values are the dated maxima the CSV independently yields. Both peaks also appear in the narrower Test-C CSV build/data/fed_swaps/SWPT_2007_2021.csv (lines 104, 701).
peak_values.absolute_series_max: 583135 (Millions of USD) on 2008-12-17. This is also the maximum of the entire series. Confirms the ~$583bn Dec-2008 GFC peak. AGREES with Test C's quoted $583,135mn on 2008-12-17.
peak_values.covid_2020_episode_max: 448946 (Millions of USD) on 2020-05-27 (maximum over Jan-Dec 2020). Confirms the ~$449bn 2020 COVID peak. AGREES with Test C's quoted $448,946mn on 2020-05-27.
peak_values.current_value: 35 (Millions of USD) on 2026-06-24 — near-zero, undistorted, consistent with the H.4.1 current release ($35 million, week ended June 24, 2026).
peak_values.units: Millions of U.S. dollars (VERIFIED from FRED /data/SWPT metadata: Units 'Millions of U.S. Dollars').

units: Millions of U.S. dollars. VERIFIED from FRED /data/SWPT metadata table (2026-06-29).
known_frictions: AGGREGATE-ONLY ON THE GROUNDABLE ENDPOINT: SWPT / H.4.1 'Central bank liquidity swaps' is a single system-aggregate figure; the H.4.1 release (incl. table 1A) does NOT disaggregate by counterparty central bank (verified from the release page 2026-06-29). A per-counterparty-CB (bilateral) breakdown would require the NY Fed Markets operation-results / API, whose machine-readable availability could not be confirmed here (UNVERIFIED). So the matrix can ground the Fed-injection system total but cannot, as verified, distribute it bilaterally across foreign central banks from a confirmed machine source.

STRESS-STATE-ONLY MATERIALITY: at the 2025 reference snapshot the balance is ~$35mn (near-zero), so omitting the series does NOT distort the present-day matrix LEVEL. The series matters for the STRESS-STATE / dynamics dimension (the circuit-breaker that acts under stress), not for the current-snapshot level (mirrors Test C scope_caveat (1)).

DOUBLE-COUNTING CAUTION vs BIS LBS: the Fed swap line is a USD-vs-foreign-central-bank bilateral that settles into foreign-bank dollar funding, overlapping conceptually with BIS LBS USD bank liabilities. Wiring it into the matrix requires care not to double-count the same dollar funding on both the Fed-injection leg and the BIS bank-liability leg (Test C scope_caveat (2)). This is a DP2/DP3 modeling concern flagged here as a known friction, not resolved in this contract.

FRED /series/ HTML 403: the human-facing https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/SWPT page returns HTTP 403 via the proxy; metadata was grounded equivalently from the FRED /data/ text endpoint and CSV and from the federalreserve.gov H.4.1 release page. An automated ingestion pipeline should use the CSV/data endpoints rather than the /series/ HTML page.

NY Fed pages anti-bot / JS-rendered: newyorkfed.org/markets/central-bank-swap-arrangements 403'd (WebFetch) and 302-redirected (curl); the desk-operations page and Markets-API doc are JS shells with no swap data/endpoints in static HTML. Confirming per-counterparty data therefore needs a JS-capable fetch or the api with the correct endpoint path (not obtained here).
tier_basis: Tier A for the SYSTEM-AGGREGATE series: structure (H.4.1 line item + FRED SWPT identity), frequency (Weekly, As of Wednesday), units (Millions of U.S. Dollars), coverage (2002-2026 weekly, spanning both 2008 and 2020), and both stress peaks ($583,135mn on 2008-12-17; $448,946mn on 2020-05-27, computed from the fetched CSV) were each read from official (federalreserve.gov H.4.1) or official-redistributed (FRED) endpoints on 2026-06-29, not from memory. The tier applies to the aggregate series only; the per-counterparty-central-bank breakdown is UNVERIFIED (could not be machine-confirmed from the NY Fed) and is explicitly NOT tier-A-grounded.

fetched_evidence.h41_current_line: From https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h41/current/ (2026-06-29): line label 'Central bank liquidity swaps' = $35 million; release week ended June 24, 2026; no counterparty breakdown (single aggregate; table 1A does not disaggregate).
fetched_evidence.fred_metadata: From https://fred.stlouisfed.org/data/SWPT (2026-06-29, HTTP 200): 'Assets: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Central Bank Liquidity Swaps: Wednesday Level' | Release: 'H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances' | Frequency: 'Weekly, As of Wednesday' | Units: 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' | Seasonal Adjustment: 'Not Seasonally Adjusted'.
fetched_evidence.csv_window: first row 2002-12-18,0 ; first non-zero 2007-12-26,14000 ; last row 2026-06-24,35 ; 1228 data rows. PERSISTED to build/data/fed_swaps/SWPT_2002_2026.csv (re-checkable from disk).
fetched_evidence.nyfed_statement: From https://www.newyorkfed.org/markets/desk-operations/central-bank-liquidity-swap-operations (2026-06-29, HTTP 200), verbatim: 'The results of the central bank liquidity swap operations and small value exercises of the central bank liquidity swap lines are published on a weekly basis when conducted.'

unverified: Per-counterparty-central-bank breakdown (amounts outstanding by foreign central bank — ECB, BoJ, SNB, BoE, BoC, etc.) as a CONFIRMED machine-readable file/endpoint: the H.4.1/SWPT aggregate carries no counterparty split (verified), and the NY Fed central-bank-swap-arrangements page (403/302), the desk-operations page (JS shell), and the Markets-API doc (JS shell, no swap endpoint in static HTML) did not yield a confirmable per-counterparty data source in this environment. Needs NY Fed Markets API / operation-results pages with a JS-capable fetch.

Federal Reserve's exact stated H.4.1 release-schedule and publication-lag policy (only empirically inferred ~few-day lag from the 2026-06-24 week-ended date being current on 2026-06-29; the release-calendar/policy page was not read).
FRED /series/SWPT HTML page content (HTTP 403 via proxy); grounded equivalently from /data/ text endpoint, CSV, and the H.4.1 release page instead.
Whether the FRED JSON/REST API (api.stlouisfed.org/fred) requires an API key for this series (not exercised; CSV sufficed).
notes: Grounded on 2026-06-29 against FRED (CSV + /data/ metadata) and the primary Federal Reserve H.4.1 current release. Frequency Weekly (As of Wednesday), units Millions of U.S. Dollars, coverage 2002-12-18 through 2026-06-24 spanning both 2008 and 2020, and both stress peaks (583,135mn on 2008-12-17 — the series max; 448,946mn on 2020-05-27) were each read from publisher/redistributor endpoints and, for the peaks, recomputed independently from the fetched CSV (they AGREE with Test C, not copied from it). This source fills the official-sector cross-border dollar-PROVISION (Fed-injection) gap that Resolution Test C established was absent from all 13 prior DP1 sources. It is groundable as a SYSTEM AGGREGATE (tier A); the per-counterparty-central-bank breakdown that would make it a true bilateral FWTW cell is UNVERIFIED and needs the NY Fed. Per the project scope, this contract is a data-grounding artifact ONLY: it makes no F4 / identification / separability claim, and the double-counting-vs-BIS-LBS and stress-state-only caveats are recorded as known frictions for downstream DP2/DP3 to handle, not resolved here.
